title: More Developers =/= Faster? Why Teams DON'T Scale! #shorts
summary: Adding more developers to engineering teams does not necessarily increase velocity; communication overhead and coordination complexity create diminishing returns. Effective scaling requires optimized team structure beyond headcount growth.
writeup: This short argues that adding developers to engineering teams often lowers productivity rather than raising it, because communication overhead and coordination complexity grow as headcount increases. The takeaway: effective scaling depends on strategic team design, not simply hiring more people.
